entry.title: GRC Tuesdays: What’s New in SAP solutions for Three Lines, Q1 2024 to Q2 2024
entry.content:
Image generated by DALL·E 3

We are once again back with our now regular “What’s New” blogs for SAP solutions for Three Lines (i.e. SAP Process Control, SAP Risk Management, SAP Audit Management and SAP Business Integrity Screening). As is now an old habit, Marie-Luise Wagener-Kirchner and Paul Petraschk from SAP GRC Product Management have shared their inputs and you will be able to discover below the new features delivered over these last few months.

This blog is only a short summary of selected enhancements delivered. But you will be able to find more details by following the links are mentioned at the end of this post.

 

Analytics for Audit Management

 

Getting to the relevant information quickly is what is important for most users. And auditors are not exempt from this finding – no pun intended!

With 13 analytical charts, the re-designed Audit Management Overview app together with the three new SAP Fiori apps: Analyze Audit Engagements, Analyze Findings, Analyze Action Plans, provide a powerful data visualization and analysis tool to help audit teams track their key metrics and gain insights into the internal auditing activities across the organization.

Furthermore, these screens are not only information display, they provide direct access to the underlying data with the click on a mouse.

Some examples of the analytical cards delivered include:

  • Audits by phase, by audit lead or audit manager, by attribute (status, type, group), by country or region
  • Audits with reports issues in the last 2 years
  • Overdue or upcoming audits
  • Open findings
  • Escalated or overdue action plans
  • … and more!

These visualizations help streamline the analysis, but also empower users with deeper insights into data for audit, finding, and action plan including visual representation and breakdowns.

Overview of the new embedded analytics in SAP Audit Management:

But wait, there’s more!

By default, the Audit Management Overview screen shows all the cards. That being said, users can personalize their overview page very easily and hide the cards they don't need.

From the “Manage Cards” option – the three dots at the top of all cards, they can simply use the toggles to hide/unhide cards. It’s that simple!

And, with the dimensions and filters, they can of course change the display, or the scope as they see fit.

In addition, SAP delivers out of the box “views” – in essence sets of predefined filtering criteria – but users can define their own filters and save them as custom views. These new views are then added to the list of standard delivered ones, and are applied as soon as they are selected. They can also be used as a default display if the user chooses.

More details and videos can be found on the dedicated page: - Audit Analytics | SAP Help Portal

 

Business Impact Analysis

 

Supporting Business Continuity Management requirements with planning, strategy definition, documentation, business impact analysis, as well as business continuity and disaster recovery plans has been a request from our SAP Risk Management customers for quite some time.

And you may recall that I advocated for the inclusion of Business Continuity Management within Three Lines for a long time (cf. for instance GRC Tuesdays: Is Business Continuity a Separate Activity?).

Well, I am very glad to announce that the first steps in this direction have been taken, and that SAP GRC Product Management and Engineering colleagues have released the first building block with the Business Impact Analysis capability in SAP Risk Management.

This module introduces a new option to classify an activity (or subprocess) as “Critical”. Subsequently, business continuity metrics can be maintained: Recovery Time Objective, Recovery Objective Point, and Criticality Level. From there, a Business Impact Analysis (BIA) survey can be run to collect inputs from expert stakeholders.

This new capability also comes with a Business Impact Analysis list report, and a dedicated response type.

This will help companies increase resilience by providing additional insights on operational risk and business continuity requirements.

Overview of the new Business Impact Analysis in SAP Risk Management:

 

More business continuity related features are planned as you will be able to see on the roadmap for SAP Risk Management, so keep an eye out for the next blogs as we detail the new capabilities:

 

More Information

 

As mentioned in introduction, these are only some of the enhancements delivered recently. For more details on what’s new, I would recommend having a look at the relevant sections of the dedicated Help Portals:

  • What's New in SAP Process Control
  • What’s New in SAP Risk Management
  • What’s New in SAP Audit Management
  • What’s New in SAP Business Integrity Screening

Previous “What’s New” blogs also provide some details on prior enhancements delivered. You will be able to find them at the following locations:

  • GRC Tuesdays: What’s New in SAP solutions for Three Lines, Q3 2023 to Q1 2024
  • GRC Tuesdays: What’s New in SAP solutions for Three Lines, Q2 2023 to Q3 2023
  • GRC Tuesdays: What’s New in SAP Process Control and SAP Risk Management, Q1 2023 to Q2 2023

I look forward to reading your thoughts and comments either on this blog or on Twitter @TFrenehard and stay tuned for our next “What’s New” blog!

And if you are interested in learning more about SAP solutions for Governance, Risk, and Compliance, feel free to fill-in the demo request form!

entry.title: Analyze Network Trace for SAP Asset and Service Manager using SAP Mobile Backend Tools
entry.content:
SAP Mobile Services provides an end to end network trace capability to record the network traffices and payload data to be analyze in order to find issues while using the mobile apps through SAP Mobile Services. Please refer to https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/tracing/admin/trace.html for more details about SAP Mobile Services network trace.

SAP Service and Asset Manager is a mobile asset management application that supports maintenance, service and safety technicians, as well as inventory management features. Please refer https://help.sap.com/docs/service-asset-manager for more details about SAP Service and Asset Manager.

In this blog post, we will show you how to analyze network trace for SAP Service and Asset Manager using SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools in order to find out issues while using the app to access the SAP Backend Systems.

As IT consultants if your users who are using SAP Service and Asset Manager report issues to you, you can record the network trace activities using mobile services cockpit according to the instructions from mobile services network trace activities. In order to analyze the network trace data after the recording has been complete, you need to a network trace tool to analyzer the har file.

If you use Google chrome for example to open the network trace har file for SAP Service and Asset Manager mobile application, you will see a UI like below:

There are some limitations in Chrome used to analyze the network trace data for SAP Asset and Service mobile app:

  • There is no correlation view for the requests. SAP Mobile Services uses correlation ID to link the different stages of the same request.
  • SAP Asset and Service Manager uses lots of batch requests on mobile services side, and the user can't view the details of the each individual batch requests.
  • Also the user can't see the overall batch response payload at all in chrome.

In order to address the above issues, SAP Mobile Backend Tools offers a network trace analyzer to better visualize the network trace har file generated by SAP Mobile Services.

First download and install SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools in Visual Studio Code. After open the folder where the network trace har file is located, right click your network trace har file and select "Open With" context menu item in Visual Studio Code:

Select "MBT: Network Trace Analyzer":

The network trace for SAP Asset and Service Manager will be opened by Mobile Backend Tools:

By default, Mobile Backend Tools network trace analyzer only shows "Path", "Status", "Size" and "Time" columns in the UI, but you can configure more visible columns by clicking the filter like below:

Now we can do some analysis using Mobile Backend Tools. Open one of the batch requests like below:

Go to the "Payload" tab, you will see the request details:

and the response details:

And you can also view the response details in each individual batch request:

Mobile Backend Tools network trace analyzer also offers a correlation view so that all the records with the same correlation ID are grouped together to be treated as different stages for the same request:

and in this correlation view, it's very straightforward to see the different stages underneath of each request:

As a conclusion, in this blog post, we demonstrated how to analyze the network trace har file generated by SAP Mobile Services for SAP Asset and Service Manager mobile app using SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools network trace analyzer that includes specific optimization for har file in mobile services specific scenarios.

entry.title: Re: Calling APIs from S/4 to SF/EC using Cloud Connector
entry.content:
The SCC needs to be installed inside the same LAN (or even on the same host) as the HTTP server on which you want to call the APIs. It then opens a secure tunnel (like a VPN) from the server to the BTP. So the basic question is: can you install additional software like the SCC on the SF and EC systems?

If yes, calling the APIs via SCC is a matter of simple configuration, if no, I don't see a way how the SCC can be used here.

entry.title: Re: Introducing the Background Processing Framework
entry.content:
Hi @Molle,

thanks for your elaboration. I will definetly continue using the bgPF for this, let alone for the cloud-readiness and the unit-tests.

We were just searching for a way to execute illegal statements (e.g. SAP-functions that execute a SUBMIT or CALL FUNCTION IN BACKGROUND TASK) synchronously, being able to provide immediate feedback whether an operation was successful in a RAP-app.

I'm aware that this is exclusively an issue in on-prem/private cloud systems, though. If i had a wish-list however, i would be glad to see an easier way to pass the bgPF-process from modify-phase to save-phase as this seems to be necessary in every use within RAP.

entry.title: TMS - Use Transport Management Service (TMS) API
entry.content:
Series – TMS:

  • Transport Content Archives on Local File Systems with Transport Management Service (TMS)
  • SAP CI/CD Integration with Transport Management Service (TMS)
  • Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CI/CPI) Content with Transport Management Service (TMS) and Content Agent Service (CAS)
  • Use Transport Management Service (TMS) API

Create a service instance of TMS with Standard plan, then create a service key:

 

Use service key details to obtain OAuth token to access TMS APIs:

Access Token URL: <url>/oauth/token
Client ID: <clientid>
Client Secret: <clientsecret>

Let's take some examples for your reference, for other APIs, please see: SAP Business Accelerate Hub - Cloud Transport Management API.

 

Example - Get versions

Method: GET

URL: <uri>/versions

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Response: 

{
    "supportedVersions": [
        {
            "version": "1",
            "path": "v1"
        },
        {
            "version": "2",
            "path": "v2"
        }
    ]
}

Example - Get nodes

Method: GET

URL: <uri>/v2/nodes

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Response: 

{
    "nodes": [
        {
            "id": 3,
            "name": "DEV_NODE",
            "uploadAllowed": true,
            "notificationEnabled": false,
            "forwardMode": "AUTO",
            "importDisabled": false,
            "importDisabledReason": "",
            "targets": [],
            "virtual": false
        },
        {
            "id": 6,
            "name": "PROD_NODE",
            "uploadAllowed": false,
            "notificationEnabled": false,
            "forwardMode": "AUTO",
            "importDisabled": false,
            "importDisabledReason": "",
            "targets": [
                {
                    "id": 7,
                    "contentType": "MTA",
                    "destinationName": "TMSTarget_CPICN40",
                    "importOptions": {
                        "strategy": "default"
                    }
                }
            ],
            "virtual": false
        }
    ]
}

Example - Get destinations

Method: GET

URL: <uri>/v2/destinations

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Example - Get transport request

Method: GET

URL: <uri>/v2/nodes/<nodeId>/transportRequests?status=<status>

nodeId: Get from API <uri>/v2/nodes

status:

  • in - Initial
  • ru - Running
  • re - Repeatable
  • su - Succeeded
  • sk - Skipped
  • wa - Warning
  • er - Error
  • fa - Fatal
  • de - Deleted

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Response: 

{
    "transportRequests": [
        {
            "id": 305,
            "status": "initial",
            "archived": false,
            "position": 9,
            "createdBy": "c7cab313-bfcb-4eac-9386-08a841ee9d57",
            "createdByNamedUser": "377275f0-acbc-445e-8bff-1514da41db9f",
            "createdAt": "2024-06-04T06:24:14.707Z",
            "queuedAt": "2024-06-04T06:24:15.099Z",
            "description": "TiaTesttoTransportPackage - sample",
            "origin": "DEV_NODE",
            "entries": [
                {
                    "id": 308,
                    "storageType": "FILE",
                    "contentType": "MTA",
                    "uri": "299"
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}

Example - Import

Method: POST

URL: <uri>/v2/nodes/<nodeId>/transportRequests/import

Header: 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'

Body:

{
  "namedUser": "Tia",
  "transportRequests": [
    305
  ]
}

Get transport request ID from the APIs like <uri>/v2/nodes/<nodeId>/transportRequests?status=<status>

Response: 

{
    "actionId": 280,
    "monitoringURL": "/v2/actions/280"
}

Example - Upload file from local to TMS

Method: POST

URL: <uri>/v2/files/upload

Header:

  • 'Authorization': 'Bearer <xxxxxx>'
  • 'file': file.zip
  • 'namedUser': 'Tia'

Useful Links

Help Portal - Integration of SAP Cloud Transport Management Using APIs

SAP Business Accelerate Hub - Cloud Transport Management API

SAP Business Accelerate Hub - Cloud Transport Management Version Endpoint API

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio - License - Access
entry.content:
Hi Geschu,

It's great you chose SAP Signavio Process Insights to get wide and quick insights into your process performance, to better understand where and how to improve.
SAP Signavio Process Insights is a SaaS solution, running on SAP Business Technology Platform.


We have created a detailed onboarding guide so that you can connect your backend system with SAP Signavio Process Insights fully on your own.
During this onboarding process, you need to create a SAP BTP subaccount (in your global account) to provision SAP Signavio Process Insights there.
You are free to do everything manual or use the "booster" that automates and speeds up admin steps for you.
How to use the booster is also part of the documentation on help.sap.com, see here: https://help.sap.com/docs/BPI/f5642e5272c1465986661c763b56213d/96d61f7482bc478990545e7237a288a1.html

Best,
Robert

either you use wave and create WT's for the wave, resulting into one single WO (depending on WOCR and Activity Areas, of course).

entry.title: Get Ready for a Data and AI Revolution: What's Hot at SAP SAPPHIRE 2024 - My personal picks!
entry.content:
Why SAP is Focusing on AI

SAP's strategic pivot towards AI is part of a comprehensive plan aimed at positioning the company for future growth. This initiative includes a significant investment in AI technologies to streamline operations and enhance efficiency. The creation of a dedicated AI growth area under the leadership of Dr. Philipp Herzig, SAP’s new Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer, underscores the centrality of AI in SAP’s future strategy. This move is designed to accelerate the development and deployment of AI solutions that can drive significant business value for SAP’s customers​ (SAP News Center)​​. You can find Dr. Herzig's session at SAPPHIRE here.

The Importance of SAP Datasphere

SAP Datasphere plays a crucial role in this AI-driven landscape. It represents a comprehensive data management solution that enables businesses to manage, integrate, and utilize their data more effectively. SAP Datasphere simplifies complex data environments by creating a unified data fabric, allowing organizations to access and leverage their data seamlessly. This capability is essential for maximizing the value of AI applications, which rely on high-quality, integrated data to function optimally​.

Datasphere's recent innovations, including the introduction of a knowledge graph and enhanced integration features, make it a pivotal tool for businesses looking to harness the power of data and AI. These advancements help companies transform their data into actionable insights, driving better decision-making and operational efficiency.

With close to 130 sessions at SAPPHIRE, the content can be a bit overwhelming. Below I've listed my top 10 sessions you should attend in my view.

Key Sessions on Data and AI

  1. Data in the Age of AI

    • Speakers: Irfan Khan, Dan Yu, Peter Howland
    • Overview: Discover how to maximize data value across SAP and third-party systems, with insights from Hershey's data transformation journey.
    • Session ID: BTP189

  2. Bringing AI from Prototype to Production

    • Speaker: Walter Sun
    • Overview: Learn about the journey of AI from prototype to production, focusing on identifying valuable AI applications and overcoming implementation challenges.
    • Session ID: TRE307

  3. Business Data Fabric: The Future of Data and AI

    • Speaker: Kaiser Larsen
    • Overview: Explore how SAP Datasphere simplifies data landscapes and empowers organizations.
    • Session ID: BTP151v

  4. SAP Datasphere: Looking Forward

    • Speaker: Torsten Ammon
    • Overview: Gain insights into new features and innovations in SAP Datasphere.
    • Session ID: BTP113

  5. Navigating the AI Era with SAP: Insights from the Executive Suite

    • Speakers: Andreas Welsch, Julia White, Michael Nilles, Walter Sun
    • Overview: Exclusive perspectives on the future of AI with SAP Business AI solutions.
    • Session ID: BAI220v

  6. Experience SAP Business AI in Action

    • Speaker: Richard Grandpierre
    • Overview: Demonstration of the Joule copilot and its impact on efficiency and decision-making.
    • Session ID: BAI900v

  7. Managing Your AI Risk: Responsible AI

    • Speaker: Dr. Bettina Laugwitz
    • Overview: Learn about mitigating AI bias and ensuring trust, privacy, and security.
    • Session ID: TRE312

  8. Learn About SAP's Responsible AI Practices

    • Speaker: Dr. Bettina Laugwitz
    • Overview: Understand SAP's commitment to ethical AI innovations.
    • Session ID: BAI206v

  9. Discover Recent and Upcoming AI Innovations for Business Functions

    • Speaker: Kai Muehlbauer
    • Overview: Learn about the Joule copilot AI Foundation and upcoming AI capabilities.
    • Session ID: BAI202

  10. Unlock the Potential of Your Data Assets: Data-Driven Decision-Making

    • Speaker: Frank Gundlich
    • Overview: Leverage technology and innovation to reshape decision-making processes.
    • Session ID: PAR309v

Conclusion

SAP SAPPHIRE 2024 is a great event for those interested in the latest advancements in Data and AI and SAP is going all in. With a diverse array of sessions, attendees will gain valuable insights into how these technologies can transform their businesses. Don't miss the opportunity to explore these sessions and stay ahead in the rapidly evolving landscape of Data and AI.

It's free, go for it.

For more information and to register for the event, visit the SAP SAPPHIRE 2024 event page and the virtual event page

entry.title: How to work with Logprobs that come from the Open AI models in the SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Logprobs

If you are familiar with logits and probability distributions, but curious about log probs, this notebook is for you.

The source notebook can be found here: https://github.com/yaoshiang/logprobs/blob/main/Logprobs.ipynb

Why do we need to understand log-probs?

Many LLM APIs now expose log-probs of the tokens it generates. This is a high resolution estimate of confidence of the model.

Most importantly, you can use these probabilities to estimate the probability or confidence of a model when trying to judge its calibration.

For example, this is the official Open AI cookbook for working with log probs, and the log-probs allow you to use an LLM as a calibrated classifier:

https://cookbook.openai.com/examples/using_logprobs

The basics: sigmoid and softmax.

Before we get into log-probs, let's cover some basics.

Why do we use sigmoid and softmax as activation functions for classifiers?

Becuase they turn the output of a neural network model, which are in the range (-inf, inf), into the range (0, 1). And we can interpret numbers in the range (0, 1) as percentages or probabilities.

The outputs of a neural network before applying sigmoid or softmax are called logits.

Sigmoid

Let's make sure we can believe the statement that sigmoid turns logits, or numbers in the range (-inf, inf), into numbers in the range (0, 1). Let's pick some corner case numbers, run sigmoid, and see if the output is indeed in the range (0,1).

Look good! The logits do indeed get squished into the range (0, 1). This means that for the numbers that we get out of a neural network, we can run the sigmoid function and interpret it as a 1% chance, 27% chance, 50% chance, 73% chance, and 99% chance of being a positive.

Softmax

Softmax takes a set of logits and like sigmoid, squishes them into the range (0,1). It has another important attribute: the sum of the numbers will be 1.0, aka 100%. Let's test that empirically.

Suppose the output of a model classifying dogs, cats, and birds is 2.0, 1.0, and -1.0. Those clearly don't sum to one. And one of the values is negative! This is definitely not a probability distribution.

But after running softmax, we do get a probably distribution: each value is between zero and one, and the sum of 71%, 26%, and 4% is indeed 100%.

Softmax is invariant to scalar shifts.

Now let's see what happens if we shift these logits by a constant amount. Let's add 10 to all the logits.

 

Interesting! The output is the same! The fancy way of saying this is that the softmax function is invariant to constant shifts in the input logits.

Numerical Instability

However, just because softmax is mathematically invariant to constant shifts, doesn't mean we can go crazy when actually doing math on silicon. A 32 bit floating point number has about 7 significant digits (ask ChatGPT about mantissa and exponent if you wanna learn more).

So if we add big number, like 10,000,000, the computer will round away the 2, 1 and -1 and just see 10,000,000. And running softmax of a vector of three numbers, all of which are the same, will result in a uniform distribution: 33.3%, 33.3%, 33.3%.

The problem actually gets worse than that. When training a neural network, we run an algorithm called back propagation, which requires that we calculate gradients of a function. Gradients are the first-order derivative - the slope of a line. If the line is really flat, your gradients will round down to zero and your model won't train. If the line is really vertical, your gradients will approach infinity.

Unfortunately, the softmax function uses exponents, and the loss function of a model uses logs. Both of these functions can get very vertical and very flat at the extremes. This means that for the shifted logits, even if they should give the same numbers after running softmax, due to numerical instability, you'll instead get a NaN or inf.

Let's run a simple backprop algorithm in pytorch to prove this.

Notice that the gradients from the big logits (102 and 101) are NaN, or Not A Number. This means the values overflowed, which would ruin any neural network training.

Notice that the gradients from the big logits (102 and 101) are NaN, or Not A Number. This means the values overflowed, which would ruin any neural network training.

Diving into the math to find the numerically stable log-softmax function.

We are going to have to dig into the greek letters to figure out how to stabilize this math.

Softmax is defined as:

Where 𝑧_𝑖 is the logit for class 𝑖.

Notice all those exponents... running 𝑒^101 is what caused our previous code to overflow into NaNs.

Crossentropy is the loss function for a probability distribution, based on the basic theory of Maximum Likelihood Estimation which underpins neural network training. Crossentropy is defined as:

Where 𝑦𝑖 is really just a switch that turns on a specific probability 𝑝𝑖 for the true label (e.g. dog), and 𝑝𝑖 is the output of the softmax function.

Combining the two equations allows us to calculate crossentropy on a logit:

We know that the log(𝑎𝑏)=log(𝑎)−log(𝑏), and log(𝑒^𝑥)=𝑥 so we can adjust the above to

 Let's look deeper inside the key of that equation: it appears to be just a constant shift of all the logits, 𝑧 _1,𝑧_2,𝑧_3,...,𝑧_𝑘. In fact, that equation is the log-softmax of the logits.

We have freed the logits 𝑧_1,𝑧_2,𝑧_3,...,𝑧_𝑘 from that nasty log-exp combo, but, we haven't solved the calculation of the right hand side of the equation. We still see the log of the sum of the exponent of a series of numbers. Are we still stuck taking the exponent of big numbers?

Luckily, we have the log-sum-exp trick to stabilize that part of the equation.

Log-Sum-Exp

Anytime you are taking the log of the sum of the exponents, know that you can factor out some of the big numbers (e.g 𝑒^101) using the log-sum-exp technique.

First, we can immediately subtract a big number 𝑀 from all the logits, then multiply the resulting value by 𝑒𝑀.

 Then, we find the log of a product of two values, and can move that to be the sum of two logs.

And we know the log of an exponent of a value is just the value... 

so we have effectively reduced the scale of our logits while adding just a constant value to the log-sum-exp of the reduced logits. Voila! Numerical stability.

The log-sum-exp trick uses the maximum M, but we need a specific M that generates the exact log-probs - but since our numbers are numerically stable, we can calculcate the log-sum-exp exactly a second time without using this trick, to essentially get a second 𝑀 value to add to the logits to find the log-probs.

Nice Properties of Log-Probs

The log prob output by APIs like Open AI are really just the logits shifted by a constant amount so that they have some nicer properties than raw logits.

Since probabilities are always between zero and one, the log-prob will always be less than zero. A high confidence prediction would have a prob of say 0.999, yielding a log-prob of something like -1e-3. A low confidence prediction would have a log prob that looks something like -0.5.

If you want to calculate the actual probability of a specific token, just take the exponent of the log-prob. No need for worrying about the other [30,521] possible tokens that the model output to calculate a sum - a single log-prob for a single token is all you need to know the probability of that token.

Log probs always can be flowed into a loss function almost directly, without need for logs or exponents or crossentropy or softmax. The loss function for this in pytorch is

https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/generated/torch.nn.NLLLoss.html

Testing log probs

Let's try to prove some of the math above. Let's set up some logits, calculate the log-softmax. We see that indeed, all the values are negative.

Now let's see if the logits and the log-probs output the same probability distribution. Yup, that checks out too! 

Finally, let's prove that we can calculate the probabilities simply by running the exp function, not the softmax, when we have the log_probs. Once again, things look good. 

 Finally, let's make sure that this is indeed numerically stable. Can we get good gradients with big numbers? Recall that we previously ran a naive approach to calculcating this gradient and got NaN for gradients.

Yup! We got good gradients despite large logits! 

SAP Generative AI Hub

If you're an SAP customer, you may be accessing Open AI models through SAP Generative AI Hub.

According to the documentation there, when you access Open AI models there, you are actually accessing Azure Open AI models and indeed, the SAP documentation points you to the Azure Open AI docs.

And Azure Open AI, like the direct Open AI API, gives you the ability to access the log-probs outputted by an LLM like GPT-4o. With those logprobs, you can build calibrated classifiers and maybe even double check if your chatbot answers aren't as good as they could be.

Advanced loss functions in TensorFlow

If you've been programming in Tensorflow, you might be a little worried right now - you've been activating your neural networks with Softmax and then using the categorical_crossentropy loss function, just like you were taught in that basic ML course. Were you risking numerical instability?

TF/Keras ignores what you implemented and operates directly on the logits. See this code block:

https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/e7e2b655d573de99c7c7fd2ceb5659f57fb2d908/tensorflow/python/keras/backend.py#L4762

Then, when it's operating on the logits, it uses the log_softmax function to apply the constant shift of the logits by the numerically stable implementation of log-sum-exp.

https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/e7e2b655d573de99c7c7fd2ceb5659f57fb2d908/tensorflow/python/ops/nn_ops.py#L4149C8-L4149C49

entry.title: Newly added approver unable to see old pending PRs for approval
entry.content:
A newly added approver in the approval group Category Manager - IT Software cannot see previous pending PRs for approval in their To-do list. 

I want to confirm the expected behavior below.

  1. If the approval flow of the PR is already Active for approval of Category Manager - IT Software, then the newly added users in this group will not be able to see it in their To-do list.
  2. Suppose the approval flow is pending approval of other approvers before the approval of the said group. In that case, the newly added user can see the PRs in their To-do list once the approval flow is Active for approval of the said group.
  3. Or may we know the standard behavior of the system for those newly added users in the group?

We also want to know how to trigger those PRs that are not in included in their To-do list, however, when they checked the PR manually they can able to see the Approve button into it.

Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Newly added approver unable to see old pending PRs fo...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

My name is Lisa from the MM-PUR team. Let me explain how the workflow approver determination works:

When the workflow(traditional or flexible workflow) is triggered, the system will determine the approvers. In your case they are determined based on the category IT Software. These approvers are set and assigned in the workflow log. There is no job that runs in the background to check if these have been changed/updated. They are determined at the time of workflow triggering initially and not again unless the actual document for example Purchase Order is updated and workflow is reset/retriggered. 

So, if a new approver is added in the workflow settings(category settings) and a document is already in Awaiting Approval status, the new approver will not see these documents in their Approval list. This is standard behaviour. The workflow has already been triggered and the original approver is determined. The only way to ensure the new approver now see's these documents is to forward the pending approval documents to the new approver. 

For any document that has not yet triggered the workflow, these will go to the new approver as the workflow has not yet ran and when it does the new approver will be determined as expected. 

I hope that helps answer your query. 

Kind regards,
Lisa
SAP Support

Best, Joerg

entry.title: BTP Acceleration加速服务介绍
entry.content:
SAP CSM是做什么的？

SAP部门大致分成产品研发，销售部门，原厂服务，SAP内部HR&IT等，其中销售部门又会涉及销售，售前，售后等，而SAP CSM（Customer Success Manager）客户成功顾问属于售后的一个角色。

SAP CSM主要负责云客户一对一服务，还会负责一对多的线上线下会议举办，SAP云产品赋能等，但凡是客户订阅了SAP云服务以后，不论是涉及商业合同与续约，产品功能与更新，工单创建与追溯（工单内容本身的支持隶属SAP原厂服务Customer Support部门）等都会与CSM进行直接沟通，CSM会自己构建知识库支持客户，并协同SAP内部资源比如产品研发，售前，原厂服务等给客户带来价值。

由于SAP产品线纵多，业务复杂，每个云服务都有自己的CSM，比如我是客户甲，同时购买了云产品SAP S/4HANA Cloud，SAP Successfactor，SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform)，那么SAP会根据我的云产品ACV（Annual Consumption Volume，简单来说就是每年订阅的费用)来安排对应的CSM，举例来说，如果我购买的三个云产品都订阅费用相对其他中小客户来说都体量巨大（这对跨国公司来说十分常见），那么SAP就会给我分配以上三个云产品的CSM。

SAP之所以细分每个云产品的CSM是因为SAP单独拿任何一个云产品来说都内容丰富，涉及的面十分广，一个CSM是无法胜任知晓所有业务线内容的，SAP S/4HANA隶属ERP领域模块众多就不再赘述。我目前是BTP的CSM，BTP上60+个服务我都需要知道他们的使用场景和功能，使用方法，如何快速开启和上手等，而这60+服务单独拿出任何一个比如SAP Integration Suite集成套件，就功能十分强大，需要专门的集成开发来实施，更不要说还有BTP上的云上数据库，云上数据仓库等，可以看出这对每个客户可能提出的多样问题来说都是极大的挑战，更何况还要处理商务续约等与人打交道的相关事宜。

但是对于客户来说，与众多来自SAP的员工打交道显然是效率低下和透明度低的，更何况这些云产品之间又有千丝万缕的联系，比如从S/4HANA到其他系统的集成就需要使用到BTP，因此SAP S/4HANA的CSM会兼任客户的唯一联系人，用以协调其他产品线的CSM一起为客户服务。

 

BTP Acceleration服务是什么？

回到BTP，由于BTP是一款PAAS产品，不仅涉及到业务流程，还涉及到大量的技术内容，比如集成，应用开发与自动化，数据与分析，AI等，细节可参考我之前的博客SAP业务技术平台(BTP)架构解读 ，因此需要一定的方法论来支持客户，我们叫做BTP Acceleration加速，其主要核心内容就是一系列的讲解和动手练习，让客户尤其是IT部门快速掌握BTP上某个服务（SAAS）的功能，用例以及如何快速开始。

形式上大部分采用远程连接的方式，由客户的其中一位IT同事分享屏幕操作练习，中间由Acceleration CSM来进行引导和答疑。

以下是目前的加速包内容。

可以看到针对比较重要的BTP服务，都有对应的加速包，然后根据使用场景做了分类：

  • Foundational Pack基础包：主要针对BTP上的子账户，用户权限管理，SAP云连接器，SAP云上编辑器Business Application Studio，创建简单的Fiori应用然后部署到SAP门户Build Work Zone，以及SAP 身份认证服务Identity Authentication等。这一部份根据客户的成熟程度，需要两到三次session，每次大概两个小时来完成。
  • App Dev & Auto包：主要针对BTP上的全栈开发以及SAP自动化，比如如何在BTP Cloud Foundry环境下部署一个全栈应用，如何使用SAP的机器人来满足自动化作业需求。这一部份会根据不同的BTP服务有不同的时长，比如在BTP Cloud Foundry下部署全栈应用涉及的东西较多，需要至少一个下午的时间来完成。
  • Data & Analytics包：主要针对SAP Analytics Cloud分析云和SAP云上数据仓库Datasphere来进行，由于这两个产品功能相对集中（都是针对数据处理和分析），加速包主要起入门和介绍作用。
  • Integration包：主要针对SAP Integration Suite集成套件展开，会带领客户做一个相对完整的场景，连接到S4系统，并且查询一个标准API获取产品信息。需要两次session，一次大概两个小时来完成。
  • AI包：主要针对BTP上的AI服务，比如Generative AI Hub来展示如何开启并使用RAG的方法部署一个AI聊天机器人。需要两次session，一次用以开启对应的AI服务和HANA Cloud，一次用以部署AI聊天应用。

需要注意的是，以上加速包并不一定在同一个客户上全部使用，主要是根据客户已经订阅的服务，以及客户在有了一定BTP经验或者后续项目需求的基础上进行选择性的交付。由于是来自CSM的免费服务，所以也不会有具体条目的合同签订和验收等。

总结来说，SAP的生态是SAP产品+SAP客户+SAP实施方来共同完成的，作为SAP产品方的CSM并不是起项目实施的作用，所以涉及具体实施细节，工程管理和追踪，项目测试，定制化的IT架构和技术咨询，并不在SAP BTP Acceleration的范围，并且在客户服务周期上偏起始阶段，一般是前6个月。一句话总结，SAP BTP Acceleration的目的就是带领客户入门BTP上的服务，促进客户使用BTP产品实现双赢。

entry.title: Develop a Side-by-Side CAP-Based Extension Application Following the SAP BTP Developer’s Guide
entry.content:
We are pleased to announce a new Discovery Center mission Develop a Side-by-Side CAP-Based Extension Application Following the SAP BTP Developer’s Guide  which is a sequel to our previous mission Develop a Full-Stack CAP Application Following the SAP BTP Developer’s Guide and is available in  SAP Discovery Center.

The primary objective of this mission is to demonstrate the implementation of a side-by-side SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP)-based extension application, following the recommendations outlined in the  SAP BTP Developer’s Guide . 

Through a step-by-step tutorial, one will learn how to develop a sample application called Incident Management, which serves as a practical learning tool. The Incident Management application is developed in accordance with best practices for building side-by-side extension applications using CAP and SAP Fiori.

Key feature showcased in this application include:

  • Development of a full-stack applications on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) using SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) 
  • Consuming events from SAP S/4HANA Cloud using SAP Event Mesh 
  • Integration of the application with REST APIs from SAP S/4HANA Cloud using SAP Side-by-Side Extensibility  
  • Deployment of the same application to both SAP BTP CF and SAP BTP Kyma runtime 

Business Scenario: 

In context of this mission, the Incident Management application assists a company called ACME in managing its  Customer Incidents.  ACME has call centre with support representatives(known as Processor) who handle customer incidents. When a customer reports an incident over the phone, a Processor creates a new incident on behalf of the customer. The newly created incident includes details of the conversation between the Processor and the customer. Furthermore, the Incident Management application enables support team members working on a customer's incident to provide updates in the form of conversations.

The scenario encompasses the following aspects: 

  • Development of a full stack custom extension application that operates independently from SAP S/4HANA Cloud application
  • Securely connectivity to SAP S/4 HANA  Cloud API’s 
  • Real-time communication of changes in SAP S/4HANA objects to extension application via events

OData Mock Server 

 Even if you do not have access to SAP S/4HANA Cloud system you can still try out our mission using the OData mock server as the source of API and events. It contains entities of SAP OData services with sample data required to run the mission 

The Discovery Centre Mission project board provides step-by-step guidance through which you will get a complete picture of how the end-to-end use case works. By trying out the use case you will also get a flavour of the different technologies showcased in this mission.  

Navigate to Option1: Cloud Foundry Runtime or Option 3: Kyma Runtime and Mock Server to access the tutorials for running the mission using OData mock server application. 

          

Solution Diagram 

           

Useful Links: 

  • Develop a Full-Stack CAP Application Following the SAP BTP Developer’s Guide 
  • For additional tutorials refer SAP Samples GitHub repository 
  • What is SAP BTP Developer’s Guide

entry.title: RAP Unmanaged Scenario - Creation of RAP BO does not return the %pid
entry.content:
Hello guys, I have an unmanaged scenario of a web API that has to create an equipment. I am using the RAP BO Interface for the Equipment which is I_EquipmentTP ( CDS View also released for Cloud ).

The RAP BO Interface successfully creates the Equipment, but I do not have access to the Equipment ID ( the key of the RAP BO I_EquipmentTP ) as the Equipment RAP BO has a late numbering applied. I've found the way to retrieve the key fields by using the 'CONVERT KEY' option ( ref. https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-rap-workshops/blob/main/rap6xx/rap610/exercises/ex4/README.md ) but the %PID of the successful creation is quite empty. I've spent little time to open the create implementation of the RAP BO and to my surprise I do not see a %pid assigned for the operation.

As a payload I am only passing the mandatory field of the RAP BO Interface, and later I can find the newly created Equipment if I run ( let's say read all entities, or SELECT * from ), but I cannot provide the Equipment ID directly after creation, since CONVERT KEY fails to get the newly created key.

My question is this an error on my side ( taking into consideration that the Equipment is successfully created and existing, I would say no ) or this is an expected behavior of the RAP BO and I have to do additional steps to retrieve the key?

entry.title: Re: How to select a specific session to do SAP GUI Scripting activities inside it
entry.content:
Hello again.

After a bit of "debugging" on the VBScript, I understood that the issue was only on the second set of loops (the first seemed to work fine everytime).

I also noticed that the 2 sets of loops (connections + sessions) were slightly different in terms of conditions, so I decided to standardize the second as per the first (the apparently working one).

At this point, all my tests have succeeded (including testing in 2 completely different customers), so I wonder if someone else wants to try it out.

I tried busy sessions from debugger, busy sessions when new session is opened by 'STARTING NEW TASK' and freezing the originating session and also a long running session, and all were read in the loop without issues (and excluded via .Busy property, I suppose).

Here's my VBScript working code (similar to above/example from Scripting Tracker):

 

'-Directives------------------------------------------------------------
Option Explicit


'-Sub Action------------------------------------------------------------
'-
'- Get the selected session and do the action inside it
'-
'-----------------------------------------------------------------------
Sub Action(session)

  'Insert your SAP GUI Scripting code from recorder here

  MsgBox session.findById("wnd[0]/titl").text

End Sub

'-Function GetSession---------------------------------------------------
'-
'- Procedure to determine SAP sessions, filter and show in InputBox to allow selection
'-
'-----------------------------------------------------------------------
Function GetSession(SapAppl)

	Dim CollCon, i, oCon, CollSes, j, oSes, oSesInf
	Dim sessionInfo, sessionData, sessionDataDict, windowid
	Dim counter, questionstring, SAPSession

	'Create a dictionary to hold selectable SAP sessions
	Set sessionDataDict = CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary")

	'Get SAP connections
	Set CollCon = SapAppl.Connections()
	If Not IsObject(CollCon) Then
		leave_script oSes,SapAppl
	End If

	counter = 0
	' Loop over connections
	For i = 0 To CollCon.Count() - 1

		Set oCon = SapAppl.Children(CLng(i))
		If Not IsObject(oCon) Then
			leave_script oSes,SapAppl
		End If

		Set CollSes = oCon.Sessions()
		If Not IsObject(CollSes) Then
			leave_script oSes,SapAppl
		End If

		' Loop over sessions
		For j = 0 To CollSes.Count() - 1

			Set oSes = oCon.Children(CLng(j))
			If Not IsObject(oSes) Then
				leave_script oSes,SapAppl
			End If 

			If oSes.Busy() = vbFalse Then

				Set oSesInf = oSes.Info()
				If IsObject(oSesInf) Then
					' ------------------------------------------------------------
					' 
					'  With the session info object is it possible to
					'  select a specific session which executes the
					'  activities. 
					' ------------------------------------------------------------
					'In case session/window is valid for scripting, is for a Givaudan ECC system and not an ABAP debugger session
					If oSesInf.IsLowSpeedConnection() = vbFalse Then
						windowid = oSes.findById("wnd[0]").id
						'Counter for SAP Sessions to display/choose from on InputBox
						counter = counter + 1
						'Build string for SAP session with Window Text, Transaction Code and Username
						sessionData = CLng(counter) & ". " & oSes.findById("wnd[0]").Text() & " (" & oSesInf.Transaction() & ") - " & oSesInf.User()
						'Add SAP session window ID to dictionary
						sessionDataDict.Add CLng(counter), windowid
						'Add string to complete question string
						questionstring = questionstring & sessionData & chr(13) & chr(13) 
					End If

				End If

			End If

		Next 'Loop over sessions

	Next 'Loop over connections

	'Ask the user in which SAP session the script is supposed to run
	If questionstring <> "" Then
		SAPSession = InputBox("Available SAP sessions" & chr(13) & chr(13) & questionstring,"Choose the SAP session to run script in")
		If SAPSession <> "" Then
			If (sessionDataDict.Exists(CLng(SAPSession))) Then

				'Loop over connections
				For i = 0 To CollCon.Count() - 1

					Set oCon = SapAppl.Children(CLng(i))
					If Not IsObject(oCon) Then
						leave_script oSes,SapAppl
					End If
				
					Set CollSes = oCon.Sessions()
					If Not IsObject(CollSes) Then
						leave_script oSes,SapAppl
					End If

					' Loop over sessions
					For j = 0 To CollSes.Count() - 1

						Set oSes = oCon.Children(CLng(j))
						If Not IsObject(oSes) Then
							leave_script oSes,SapAppl
						End If 

						If oSes.Busy() = vbFalse Then

							Set oSesInf = oSes.Info()
							If IsObject(oSesInf) Then
								' ------------------------------------------------------------
								' 
								'  With the session info object is it possible to
								'  select a specific session which executes the
								'  activities. 
								' ------------------------------------------------------------
								'In case session/window is valid for scripting
								If oSesInf.IsLowSpeedConnection() = vbFalse Then
									If oSes.findById("wnd[0]").id = sessionDataDict(CLng(SAPSession)) Then
										Set GetSession = oSes
									End If
								End If
							End If
						End If
					Next 'Loop over sessions
				Next 'Loop over connections
			Else 'Wrong value provided
				MsgBox "Please choose one of the available SAP sessions",16,"Error"
				leave_script oSes,SapAppl
			End If
		Else 'User cancelled and did not provide any value
			MsgBox "Please choose one of the available SAP sessions",16,"Error"
			leave_script oSes,SapAppl
		End If
	Else 'No SAP session determined to show on InputBox (none valid)
		MsgBox "No SAP session is opened." & chr(13) & "Please open an SAP session before running this script!",16,"Error"
		leave_script oSes,SapAppl
	End If

End Function

'-Function leave_script------------------------------------------
'-
'- Function to gracefully close the script: 
'-   - Re-enable SAPGui history
'-   - Detach from connected SAPGui session (if any)
'    - Close the script
'-
'-----------------------------------------------------------------------
Function leave_script(session,application)
	'Re-enable SAPGui history setting
	application.HistoryEnabled = True
	'Detach script from SAP session
	If IsObject(WScript) Then
		If IsObject(session) Then
			WScript.ConnectObject session,     "off"
		End If
		If IsObject(application) Then
			WScript.ConnectObject application, "off"
		End If
	End If
	set application = nothing
	set session = nothing
	'Quit the script
	WScript.quit
End Function

'-Sub Main--------------------------------------------------------------
'-
'- Main procedure
'-
'-----------------------------------------------------------------------
Sub Main()

  Dim SapGuiAuto, application, session

  'SAP-generated routine to connect to SAP session
  If Not IsObject(application) Then
    Set SapGuiAuto  = GetObject("SAPGUI")
    Set application = SapGuiAuto.GetScriptingEngine
    If  application.Connections.Count() = 0 Then
      MsgBox "No SAP session is opened." & chr(13) & "Please open an SAP session before running this script!",16,"Error"
      Wscript.Quit
    End If
    'Temporarily disable SAPGui history setting
    application.HistoryEnabled = vbFalse
  End If
  
  'Call function to get list of valid open SAP sessions for user to choose from
  Set session = GetSession(application)
  If Not IsObject(session) Then
    leave_script session,application
  End If
  
  Action session

End Sub

'-Main------------------------------------------------------------------
Main()

'-End-------------------------------------------------------------------

FYI, here's a "bogus preview" of the input box shown:

@stefan_schnell Thanks a lot for all your insights and thoughts, I will consider converting my scripts to Powershell instead (considering it is Windows native and recognizing that investing in VBScript is a "lost cause").

If anyone cares to test it, I'd be interested in getting your feedback.

Thanks everyone for all your help and inspiration.

entry.title: Re: Stock sorting "FIFO" (WDATU) during WT creation does...
entry.content:
🙂it was caused by an SAP EWM error in FM /SCWM/AQUA_DATA_DETERMINE

SNOTE https://me.sap.com/notes/0003127733 (and dependent one's) will fix it.

Be aware that storage type /SCWM/V_T3010-MIXGR is space by default, which means "most recent date dominates". This is an unlucky default for real life, as it makes old stock to new stock, if you just put another Sub-HU (with newer WDATU) to same Top-HU containing already a Sub-HU with older stock.

Better use /SCWM/V_T3010-MIXGR=2 to ensure that /SCWM/AQUA geds WDATU+VFDAT from the oldest physical stock

entry.title: Re: Configuring Date Ranges in Stories Reports
entry.content: So i eventually figured this out. On the Headcount report I am using As of date with the option to change the period. For all movement reports i.e. Terminations, Promotions, Hires I have added a date range using the Event Start Date, set it to YTD and provided the option to change the period looking back or forward. I eliminated the duplicates in the detail data by selecting Personal Details from the Job Info > Personal Info table and not the Position > Incumbents table. I also converted all reports to Optimized mode which has more functionality, especially with the date range option provided.

entry.title: Generate an OData Server using SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools
entry.content:
If you have an OData metadata document and you want to build an OData server from it, you can use SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools. SAP Mobile Backend Tools allows the developer to generate an OData services from an OData CSDL file and deploy the service to SAP Business Technology Platform.

In our previous blog post, we know how to add an SAP Mobile Services landscape to Mobile Backend Tools. In order to create a mobile server, you need to create a mobile backend server project first.

Run "MBT: Create Mobile Backend Server Project" command from the Visual Studio Code command palette:

You will be able to see the mobile backend server wizard:

Find an empty directory for the mobile backend server, and specify the location of the OData CSDL document:

Press "OK" button to generate the server:

Click "Open" button to open the mobile backend server project:

Go to the "mobile services" view and click "server1" node:

You will see a mobile backend server config console:

There are 4 buttons you can use with your server:

  • Build the server. If you make changes in the source code, or you change the configuration of the server, you need to rebuild your server.
  • Preview the server. Before deploying the server to the cloud, you can preview the server using a local Tomcat.
  • Deploy the server. You can deploy the server to SAP Business Technology Platform Cloud Foundry environment, but before doing that you need to login to cloud foundry first through "cf login" command.
  • Deploy the server to SAP Mobile Services. You can deploy the server to the same cloud foundry environment as the SAP Mobile Services landscape added previously, and you can also create a mobile destination to an existing mobile application in the mobile services instance.

In this blog post, we will show you how to deploy the server through the "Deploy to Mobile Services" button:

Click the "Deploy to Mobile Services" button, you will see the mobile services landscape name in the pop up window:

Select this mobile services landscape and press enter key, you will be prompted with an option whether you want to create a mobile destination:

Select "No" because we only want to deploy the service this time, and the deployment is started. Wait until the deployment has been finished:

And then you can test the deployed OData server using your browser:

 

As a summary, in this blog post we have demonstrated how to generate a mobile backend server using SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools from an OData CSDL document. We also demonstrated how to deploy the mobile backend server to the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry environment through the added mobile services instance.

entry.title: How to extract payment currency details updated for employee Bank information from SAP
entry.content:
Please suggest how to extract payment currency details updated for employee Bank information from SAP.

Currently in case of an employee Transfer or Rehire a new work agreement is created along with new SAP Personnel number and Business Partner. When banking details for the new work agreement are received they are updated against the new Business Partner created and banking data present at the Old business partner (delimited records) is overwritten (wiped out) as we are using the replication API that synchronises SAP records with external system.

There is a requirement from customer to retain/update Banking details for the Old Business Partner as well to pay out the employee expenses. For updating the employee banking information payment currency is a PERSON_NAME field that needs to be input in the API. In order to update banking details for old business partner we need to extract the Banking details from SAP and pass them in the payload.

Using the I_BusinessPartnerBank CDS we could extract Banking details of employee but payment currency was missing here. Could you please confirm how to extract the payment currency details stored for the employee banking information from SAP. Please suggest the suitable CDS Views that need to be used to extract this information from SAP.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Unsupported and won't be available :- No Linked Dimension is use for Blended Visualization
entry.content:
SAP have indicated that they won’t be supporting blended charts that don’t include the linked dimension.

There may be a number of situations where you don’t want to include the linked dimensions e.g. technical database key with no meaning to the user.

Is there an easy workaround to this e.g. hiding the extra field? 

Example :- 

Model blends:

  • SAC usage activities

  • SAC user details

linked on dimension user_id

 

In the classic Stories , we can just have dimensons = "Name " and the chart can be seen.

 

In the optimized mode ,

dimensions = name, user_id

User id needs to be included as the linked dimension .

 

Understand that SAP won't support but can you please help us with the work around to hide the User_id in the optimized mode or handle such scenarios.

but my code here, i managed to get the counted qty from INC3 table but the data duplicated. Here is my code and the outcome:


SELECT distinct
T0.ItemCode, 
T5.ItemName, 
T0.WhsCode, 
T4.DistNumber,
--SUM(T3.OnHandQty) as 'Batch Quantity',
SUM(T2.OnHandQty) as 'Total Stock Bin',
--SUM(T0.OnHand) AS 'Total Stock Warehouse',
T1.BinCode, t1.AbsEntry as 'BinEntry', t6.Quantity as 'Batch Quantity'
 
FROM 
OITW T0
INNER JOIN OBIN T1 ON T0.WhsCode = T1.WhsCode 
INNER JOIN OIBQ T2 ON T2.WhsCode = T0.WhsCode AND T1.AbsEntry = T2.BinAbs AND T0.ItemCode = T2.ItemCode
INNER JOIN OBBQ T3 ON T3.ItemCode = T0.ItemCode AND T3.BinAbs = T1.AbsEntry AND T3.WhsCode = T2.WhsCode
INNER JOIN OBTN T4 ON T4.AbsEntry = T3.SnBMDAbs
INNER JOIN OITM T5 ON T5.ItemCode = T0.ItemCode
inner join INC3 t6 on t5.ItemCode=t6.ItemCode
WHERE 
T2.OnHandQty <> 0 and t0.WhsCode='P2' and t5.ManBtchNum='Y' and t0.ItemCode='MP0742SMN013 ' --and t4.DistNumber='309677-1' --and t1.BinCode='P2-GF1'
GROUP BY 
T0.ItemCode, T5.ItemName, T0.WhsCode, 
T0.WhsCode, T1.BinCode, T4.DistNumber, t1.AbsEntry,t6.Quantity
 
 

can someone help me? i am totally stuck.

@SonTran

entry.content:
Hello Naresh,

 

You might already assess the multiple UoM functionality. then you can maintain multiple purchase/sales UoM with different length and weight and even different price per UoM for 1 item. however, inventory UoM is always only one. so if you need to track inventory stock by different UoM and its characteristics, this approach might not be feasible. 

then I think Batch can be the only feasible solution.

 

Regards
EunSeok

entry.title: Re: RAP: Side Effect for a whole table
entry.content:
@lars_kr 

Yes, it is possible to fill associated entity (the whole table) using side effect. Sample annotations from the document is below:

<Annotations Target="STTA_SALES_ORDER_WD_20_SRV.C_STTA_SalesOrderItem_WD_20Type">
     <Annotation Term="com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1.SideEffects" Qualifier="TaxAmountChanged">
          <Record>
               <PropertyValue Property="SourceProperties">
                    <Collection>
                         <PropertyPath>TaxAmount</PropertyPath>
                    </Collection>
               </PropertyValue>
               <PropertyValue Property="TargetEntities">
                    <Collection>
                         <NavigationPropertyPath>to_SalesOrder</NavigationPropertyPath>
                    </Collection>
               </PropertyValue>
          </Record>
     </Annotation>
</Annotations>

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA – Upgrade Faster – Resolving CDS view lifecycle impacts
entry.content:
Hello All,

Before we begin, I would like to quickly introduce myself. My name is Rahul Lohia and I am a Development Consultant at SAP New Zealand. I started as an ABAP developer in 2010 and now mostly focussed on S/4HANA implementations/upgrades, BTP & Fiori development. I have been part of the community for some time now but this would be my first blog post.

 

Introduction

We know that VDM (virtual data model) forms the basis for data access in SAP S/4HANA, for example, analytical applications and APIs. The VDM of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA is represented by CDS (core data services) views. However, like any complex system, CDS views can sometimes run into dependency issues that prevent them from functioning properly during an S/4HANA upgrade cycle.

Custom CDS views are often created by businesses to meet specific requirements that are not covered by standard views. However, during an upgrade there can be multiple changes. Refer to the blog ‘Upgrade Faster – Managing app lifecycle impacts on users’ by Jocelyn Dart. Custom CDS views are just one of the many impacts that can cause disruptions to the company's operations. There's also a high probability that during an UPG phase the SUM tool might throw these errors as it would be unable to activate multiple CDS views.

This blog post will detail how you can:

  • Identify the impact.
  • Analyse errors
  • Update the objects to make them compatible with the system.
  • Activate and validate.       
  • Test and document.

1. Identify the impact of CDS view lifecycle changes

It's important to understand that deprecation is a normal part of the software cycle. This is well explained in the help page Deprecated and Decommissioned CDS views. The page also explains the recommended ways for finding the deprecated CDS views Learning About Deprecated CDS Views .

Note: As of today, running an ATC(ABAP Test Cockpit) check for the relevant S/4HANA readiness may not show all the impacted CDS views. This ATC configuration may need to be modified to collect these details.

 

2. Analyse the errors 

There are a few tools available at our disposal that can assist in analysing the CDS views. The are detailed in the note 2519431 - CDS Analysis and Repair Tools

There are also a list of bugs that may be stopping the activation. Note 2421686 - CDS view (DDL SQL View) cannot be activated with RUTDDLSACT is a good reference as well.

However, most of the analysis might have to be manually carried out. Eclipse/ADT could be quite helpful in showing the problems if multiple views are checked at the same time.

 

3. Update the CDS views 

Once you have identified the deprecated or missing CDS views, you will need to update them to follow the SAP recommendations and guidelines for replacing deprecated CDS views with the new ones.

However, there could be multiple reasons why CDS views are in error:

  • Reference standard CDS view does not exist - If the referred view has been removed in the upgraded version, this might be available in the ATC result (provided a simplification item exists). There could be multiple options:
    • If a direct replacement is recommended or found then replace the view with the successor view in your custom view.
    • If a successor view does not exist you will need to create new custom CDS views to replace them. You can use the ABAP Development Tools (ADT) or SAP HANA Studio to create new CDS views and migrate the logic from the deprecated views to the new ones.
  • Standard CDS view element/field does not exist - The element/field name might have changed in the upgraded version. Use the new element in your CDS view. If the element you want do not exist, you may need to:
    • Refer an association if the field/element was removed to another view. 
    • Create a CDS view extension to bring the element that you want to use.

        Note: Be careful when using an association/extension that you do not alter the cardinality of the core view.

  • Standard CDS view association does not exist - There may be scenarios where an existing association of a standard view may not be exposed anymore or have been removed completely. You may have to:
    • Use the new association name if it exists.
    • Add the required association directly to your custom view using the correct key fields and cardinality
  • There might be cyclical dependencies if the data model is very complex. This creates extra effort in fixing the CDS views as the individual activation of CDS view may not work. It also makes it really difficult to find a starting point in fixing the issues. You can use the ADT dependency analyzer to assist in resolving the related views. You may also want to try out commenting the cyclical associations to reduce the syntax errors and narrow the problematic view. Do not forget to uncomment once the views have been adjusted and fixed.

Note: As you work through the changes it's important to consider the stability contracts of CDS views and setting them for custom views appropriately.

 

4. Activate and validate

When all the views have been changed, you can choose the 'Activate All' feature in eclipse or use the program 'RUTDDLSACT' to activate all the CDS views at one.

There might be a bit of going back and forth to get all the views active as some of them may still require corrections. Keep repeating step 2-4 until all the views are free of errors and active. Fixing these errors might also indirectly resolve some of the custom code checks in ATC. If these CDS views are used elsewhere in your codes like in calculation views, other database views or programs, they may also need to be changed accordingly. 

 

5. Test and document 

Testing is a critical step in this process. After the changes are done, thoroughly test the CDS views to make sure they are working as expected. The business processes consuming these data models also need to be tested as a dependent GW service or SAP Fiori application may be consuming the views/elements. These errors might only show up when the processes are tested rigorously. 

Make sure the changes are well documented so that a future developer can understand them and prepare themselves for any future impacts.

 

You’ll find much more on SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Other helpful links:

  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates 
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Aggregate usage data in your production system with SUSG transaction
entry.content:
Hi Jens,

this options apply only to the created snapshot, not to the usage data in the system. The usage data in the system is not deleted. A snapshot is always a copy of the usage data, and in this copy the data is deleted as described in the option(s).

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Production order confirmation via custom design
entry.content:
Hello,

We have custom program for Production orders confirmation. It uses BAPI_PRODORDCONF_CREATE_TT.
I think there is some known issue, when sometimes entries are stuck in CO1P due to posting in update mode. As advised, to avoid CO1P entries BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE can be used.
But unfortunately for us it is not an option.

>> The process we have:
1. Users confirm Production orders through custom tool
2. Background job for CORUPROC is run every 5 minutes

>> The problems we have:
1. Sometimes during confirmation users ignore the fact that Goods receipt did not happen. They see that nothing happened and post it one more time. Later CO1P is processed and we have situation, that Goods receipt were duplicated.
2. Sometimes when user is working with particular Production order and at the same time the job CORUPROC is processed using the same order. It leads to receiving entry in CO16N with error "User locks the order" which can be processed only manually.

>> So the question is how it is better to avoid the issues? If we can not re-design the program, probably we can add check giving to user the restricting message.
1. I'm not sure in which moment entries go to CO1P. Is it possible during confirmation to catch it and show to user the message, kind of: "Goods receipt is stuck in CO1P..." ?
2. If point 1 is possible, is it possible to distinguish CO1P entry from entries COGI and CO16N?
3. If it is not possible to catch CO1P entry during confirmation. Probably after confirmation, wait a few seconds and then check AFFW table and give the message if entries are found? But how to distinguish CO1P entry from entries COGI and CO16N?
4. Any other options or hints? Maybe I need to think in other direction.

Thanks.

entry.title: Where can I find learning material forSAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering Event-Driven Integration with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh learning journey

Overview
This learning journey introduces you to SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh which is an offering that complements SAP Event Mesh for more demanding scenarios. As part of SAP’s event-driven ecosystem, it is a fully managed event streaming and event management service that enables enterprise-grade event-driven architecture.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to understand the concept of an event-driven architecture, its benefits and components, such as different event types and formats. In your role as a developer, you will also get insights into the capabilities, tools and benefits of SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, and will explore the differences and commonalities to SAP Event Mesh.

Prerequisites

  • Discovering SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Developing with SAP Integration Suite

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

We appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

entry.title: Re: Incorrect DG Status in Sales Order
entry.content:
Hi Ajit,

I have unfortunately no further idea what could be missing/wrong, because this status in the Sales Order you should only get for Products which are classified as "No Dangerous Goods" or for Products which are set in the Product Master to "Compliance Relevant" = "No".

I would suggest you set up a ticket, so that the support can look on your system.

Best Regards,
Marianne
